title: Aptamer-based profiling of blood samples by APTASHAPE for diagnosis, treatment evaluation and disease severity prediction
author: Daniel Miotto Dupont
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
The poster was presented at the Danish RNA society's 7th annual meeting on RNA. Link: https://rnasociety.dk/events/previousevents .  

 Summary:  
The molecular composition of blood is a reflection of our physiology, health state, life-style etc., in many ways a highway of biological information difficult to access. We developed a novel technology, APTASHAPE, to translate this "difficult to read" language into readable digital information. 
1) APTASHAPE involves the use of panels of small biosensor molecules, 2’-fluoro protected RNA aptamers, to generate imprints of the protein and metabolite signature in a plasma sample. 
2) Patterns are identified for health conditions. 
3) Unknown samples can be profiled. 
4) Aptamers can be used for biomarker identification and prototype drug development. 

 Contact information:
